title: The Soldier
content:
Yes. Wh� do we �ll, seeing of a soldier, bless him? bless
    Our redcoats, our tars? Both these being, the greater part,
    But frail clay, nay but foul clay. Here it is: the heart,
    Since, proud, it calls the calling manly, gives a guess
    That, hopes that, makesbelieve, the men must be no less;
    It fancies, feigns, deems, dears the artist after his art;
    And fain will find as sterling all as all is smart,
    And scarlet wear the spirit of w�r th�re express.

    Mark Christ our King. He knows war, served this soldiering through;
    He of all can handle a rope best. There he bides in bliss
    Now, and s�eing somewh�re some m�n do all that man can do,
    For love he leans forth, needs his neck must fall on, kiss,
    And cry 'O Christ-done deed! So God-made-flesh does too:
    Were I come o'er again' cries Christ 'it should be this'.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Gerard Manley Hopkins delivers a powerful performance in "The Soldier"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
